args.name: image
args.desc: The image to delete the path from

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the path to delete.

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to get the current path from

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the path whose locked status should be obtained.

args.name: image
args.desc: The image the paths belongs to

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to list the paths from

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the path whose points should be listed.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the path whose tattoo should be obtained.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the SVG file to import.

args.name: merge
args.desc: Merge paths into a single vectors object. (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: scale
args.desc: Scale the SVG to image dimensions. (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to list the paths from

args.name: image
args.desc: The image in which a path will become current

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the path to make current.

args.name: name
args.desc: the name of the path whose locked status should be set

args.name: locked
args.desc: Whether the path is locked (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to set the paths in

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the path to create. If it exists then a unique name will be created - query the list of paths if you want to make sure that the name of the path you create is unique. This will be set as the current path.

args.name: ptype
args.desc: The type of the path. Currently only one type (1 = Bezier) is supported.

args.name: num-path-points
args.desc: The number of elements in the array, i.e. the number of points in the path * 3. Each point is made up of (x, y, type) of floats. Currently only the creation of bezier curves is allowed. The type parameter must be set to (1) to indicate a BEZIER type curve. Note that for BEZIER curves, points must be given in the following order: ACCACCAC... If the path is not closed the last control point is missed off. Points consist of three control points (control/anchor/control) so for a curve that is not closed there must be at least two points passed (2 x,y pairs). If (num_path_points/3) % 3 = 0 then the path is assumed to be closed and the points are ACCACCACCACC. (num-path-points >= 0)

args.name: points-pairs
args.desc: The points in the path represented as 3 floats. The first is the x pos, next is the y pos, last is the type of the pnt. The type field is dependent on the path type. For beziers (type 1 paths) the type can either be (1.0 = BEZIER_ANCHOR, 2.0 = BEZIER_CONTROL, 3.0= BEZIER_MOVE). Note all points are returned in pixel resolution.

args.name: name
args.desc: the name of the path whose tattoo should be set

args.name: tattovalue
args.desc: The tattoo associated with the name path. Only values returned from 'path_get_tattoo' should be used here

args.name: image
args.desc: The image which contains the path to stroke

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the path which should be made into selection.

args.name: op
args.desc: The desired operation with current selection { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-conicto.blurb: Extends a bezier stroke with a conic bezier spline.
gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-conicto.help: Extends a bezier stroke with a conic bezier spline. Actually a cubic bezier spline gets added that realizes the shape of a conic bezier spline.

args.name: x0
args.desc: The x-coordinate of the control point

args.name: y0
args.desc: The y-coordinate of the control point

args.name: x1
args.desc: The x-coordinate of the end point

args.name: y1
args.desc: The y-coordinate of the end point

gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-cubicto.blurb: Extends a bezier stroke with a cubic bezier spline.
gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-cubicto.help: Extends a bezier stroke with a cubic bezier spline.

args.name: x0
args.desc: The x-coordinate of the first control point

args.name: y0
args.desc: The y-coordinate of the first control point

args.name: x1
args.desc: The x-coordinate of the second control point

args.name: y1
args.desc: The y-coordinate of the second control point

args.name: x2
args.desc: The x-coordinate of the end point

args.name: y2
args.desc: The y-coordinate of the end point

gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-lineto.blurb: Extends a bezier stroke with a lineto.
gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-lineto.help: Extends a bezier stroke with a lineto.

gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-new-ellipse.blurb: Adds a bezier stroke describing an ellipse the vectors object.
gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-new-ellipse.help: Adds a bezier stroke describing an ellipse the vectors object.

args.name: angle
args.desc: The angle the x-axis of the ellipse (radians, counterclockwise)

gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-new-moveto.blurb: Adds a bezier stroke with a single moveto to the vectors object.
gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-new-moveto.help: Adds a bezier stroke with a single moveto to the vectors object.

gimp-vectors-copy.help: This procedure copies the specified vectors object and returns the copy.

gimp-vectors-export-to-file.blurb: save a path as an SVG file.
gimp-vectors-export-to-file.help: This procedure creates an SVG file to save a Vectors object, that is, a path. The resulting file can be edited using a vector graphics application, or later reloaded into GIMP. If you pass 0 as the 'vectors' argument, then all paths in the image will be exported.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the SVG file to create.

args.name: vectors
args.desc: The vectors object to be saved, or 0 for all in the image

gimp-vectors-export-to-string.blurb: Save a path as an SVG string.
gimp-vectors-export-to-string.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-vectors-export-to-file' but creates a string rather than a file. The contents are a NUL-terminated string that holds a complete XML document. If you pass 0 as the 'vectors' argument, then all paths in the image will be exported.

args.name: vectors
args.desc: The vectors object to save, or 0 for all in the image

gimp-vectors-get-image.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-image' instead.
gimp-vectors-get-image.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-image' instead.

gimp-vectors-get-linked.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-linked' instead.

gimp-vectors-get-name.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-name' instead.
gimp-vectors-get-name.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-name' instead.

gimp-vectors-get-strokes.blurb: List the strokes associated with the passed path.
gimp-vectors-get-strokes.help: Returns an Array with the stroke-IDs associated with the passed path.

gimp-vectors-get-tattoo.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.
gimp-vectors-get-tattoo.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.

gimp-vectors-get-visible.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.
gimp-vectors-get-visible.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.

gimp-vectors-import-from-file.blurb: Import paths from an SVG file.
gimp-vectors-import-from-file.help: This procedure imports paths from an SVG file. SVG elements other than paths and basic shapes are ignored.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the SVG file to import.

args.name: merge
args.desc: Merge paths into a single vectors object. (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: scale
args.desc: Scale the SVG to image dimensions. (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-vectors-import-from-string.blurb: Import paths from an SVG string.
gimp-vectors-import-from-string.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-vectors-import-from-file' but takes a string rather than reading the SVG from a file. This allows you to write scripts that generate SVG and feed it to GIMP.

args.name: string
args.desc: A string that must be a complete and valid SVG document.

args.name: length
args.desc: Number of bytes in string or -1 if the string is NULL terminated.

args.name: merge
args.desc: Merge paths into a single vectors object. (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: scale
args.desc: Scale the SVG to image dimensions. (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-vectors-is-valid.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-valid' instead.
gimp-vectors-is-valid.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-valid' instead.

gimp-vectors-new.blurb: Creates a new empty vectors object.
gimp-vectors-new.help: Creates a new empty vectors object. The vectors object needs to be added to the image using 'gimp-image-insert-vectors'.

args.name: name
args.desc: the name of the new vector object.

gimp-vectors-new-from-text-layer.blurb: Creates a new vectors object from a text layer.
gimp-vectors-new-from-text-layer.help: Creates a new vectors object from a text layer. The vectors object needs to be added to the image using 'gimp-image-insert-vectors'.

args.name: parasite
args.desc: The parasite to attach to the item

gimp-vectors-parasite-detach.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-detach-parasite' instead.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the parasite to detach from the item.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the parasite to find

gimp-vectors-parasite-list.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-parasite-list' instead.

gimp-vectors-remove-stroke.blurb: remove the stroke from a vectors object.
gimp-vectors-remove-stroke.help: Remove the stroke from a vectors object.

gimp-vectors-set-tattoo.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-tattoo' instead.

gimp-vectors-set-visible.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-visible' instead.

args.name: visible
args.desc: The new item visibility (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-vectors-stroke-flip.help: Rotates the given stroke around given center by angle (in degrees).

args.name: flip-type
args.desc: Flip orientation, either vertical or horizontal { ORIENTATION-HORIZONTAL (0), ORIENTATION-VERTICAL (1) }

args.name: axis
args.desc: axis coordinate about which to flip, in pixels

gimp-vectors-stroke-flip-free.blurb: flips the given stroke about an arbitrary axis.
gimp-vectors-stroke-flip-free.help: Flips the given stroke about an arbitrary axis. Axis is defined by two coordinates in the image (in pixels), through which the flipping axis passes.

args.name: x1
args.desc: X coordinate of the first point of the flipping axis

args.name: y1
args.desc: Y coordinate of the first point of the flipping axis

args.name: x2
args.desc: X coordinate of the second point of the flipping axis

args.name: y2
args.desc: Y coordinate of the second point of the flipping axis

gimp-vectors-stroke-get-length.blurb: Measure the length of the given stroke.
gimp-vectors-stroke-get-length.help: Measure the length of the given stroke.

args.name: precision
args.desc: The precision used for the approximation

gimp-vectors-stroke-get-point-at-dist.blurb: Get point at a specified distance along the stroke.
gimp-vectors-stroke-get-point-at-dist.help: This will return the x,y position of a point at a given distance along the stroke. The distance will be obtained by first digitizing the curve internally and then walking along the curve. For a closed stroke the start of the path is the first point on the path that was created. This might not be obvious. If the stroke is not long enough, a "valid" flag will be FALSE.

args.name: precision
args.desc: The precision used for the approximation

gimp-vectors-stroke-get-points.blurb: returns the control points of a stroke.
gimp-vectors-stroke-get-points.help: returns the control points of a stroke. The interpretation of the coordinates returned depends on the type of the stroke. For Gimp 2.4 this is always a bezier stroke, where the coordinates are the control points.

gimp-vectors-stroke-interpolate.blurb: returns polygonal approximation of the stroke.
gimp-vectors-stroke-interpolate.help: returns polygonal approximation of the stroke.

args.name: precision
args.desc: The precision used for the approximation

gimp-vectors-stroke-new-from-points.blurb: Adds a stroke of a given type to the vectors object.
gimp-vectors-stroke-new-from-points.help: Adds a stroke of a given type to the vectors object. The coordinates of the control points can be specified. For now only strokes of the type GIMP_VECTORS_STROKE_TYPE_BEZIER are supported. The control points are specified as a pair of float values for the x- and y-coordinate. The Bezier stroke type needs a multiple of three control points. Each Bezier segment endpoint (anchor, A) has two additional control points (C) associated. They are specified in the order CACCACCAC...

args.name: type
args.desc: type of the stroke (always GIMP_VECTORS_STROKE_TYPE_BEZIER for now). { VECTORS-STROKE-TYPE-BEZIER (0) }

args.name: num-points
args.desc: The number of elements in the array, i.e. the number of controlpoints in the stroke * 2 (x- and y-coordinate). (num-points >= 0)

args.name: controlpoints
args.desc: List of the x- and y-coordinates of the control points.

args.name: closed
args.desc: Whether the stroke is to be closed or not. (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-vectors-stroke-rotate.help: Rotates the given stroke around given center by angle (in degrees).

args.name: center-x
args.desc: X coordinate of the rotation center

args.name: center-y
args.desc: Y coordinate of the rotation center

args.name: vectors
args.desc: The vectors object to render to the selection

args.name: operation
args.desc: The desired operation with current selection { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }
